title: 3 reasons for optimism and 3 causes for concern as the Red Sox approach a new season
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: Red Sox's Roman Anthony, a promising young star, aims to elevate his performance after hitting .292 with 8 HRs last year.
Veteran pitcher Garrett Crochet and newcomers strengthen Red Sox rotation, aiming for a potential deep postseason run.
Risks in World Baseball Classic participation include potential injuries affecting team chemistry and preparation for the season.

title: Boston police seeking suspect in armed robbery
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Boston Police seek help identifying suspect in Jan. 22 armed robbery in Dorchester, involving multiple victims.
The robbery occurred at 4:30 p.m. near Adams and Whitten Streets, with the suspect approaching victims from behind.
Police urge anyone with information to contact detectives or use the anonymous CrimeStoppers tip line.

title: No license yet for World Cup games as Foxborough asks for public safety funding up front
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Foxborough officials demand $7.8 million in upfront public safety funding from Boston Soccer 2026 for World Cup games.
Boston Soccer 2026's lawyers propose post-event payment but commit to cover shortfalls with support from Kraft Sports and Entertainment.
Massachusetts awaits $46 million in federal funding; failure to secure funds could risk license for games at Gillette Stadium.

title: Wu to host first-ever ‘office hours’ for residents to speak with her directly
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Mayor Michelle Wu will host 'office hours' for Bostonians to discuss city issues at the Bruce C. Bolling Building.
The event will include staff from over a dozen city departments to address public concerns and provide services.
City services available include census registration, voting registration, and processing of various certificates.

title: 7 players Bruins could target at 2026 NHL trade deadline
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bruins consider trading draft picks for players like Robert Thomas and Conor Garland before NHL deadline.
Simon Nemec and Rasmus Ristolainen among potential targets to strengthen Bruins' defense.
Owens Tippett considered as top-six forward acquisition to boost Bruins' offensive capabilities.

title: Staff member at special needs residential program in Franklin accused of breaking autistic student’s arm
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Saturs Allison, 27, from Pawtucket, R.I., was indicted for allegedly breaking an autistic student's arm at Amego, Inc. in Franklin.
The incident occurred in October 2025 and was allegedly captured on security footage; Allison allegedly used improper restraint techniques.
Allison pleaded not guilty and was released on personal recognizance; he faces up to five years in prison if convicted.

title: ‘Every run is about defying cancer’: This 18-year-old is running Boston for her childhood friend
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Amanda Lewis, 18, from Charlotte, NC, is running the Boston Marathon in memory of her friend Lily, who died of brain cancer.
Lewis aims to raise funds for the Dana-Farber Cancer Institute through her participation in the marathon.
She runs to honor cancer survivors and victims, emphasizing emotional endurance as learned through long-distance running.

title: Fall River man sentenced for raping woman when she refused to take naked pictures of young daughter
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Elias Rodrigues Dos Santos Mendes sentenced to 8-10 years for rape and child pornography in Fall River Superior Court.
Dos Santos Mendes will be deported to Brazil after serving his sentence, as stated by Bristol County DA's office.
The victim linked Mendes through DNA after reporting the rape incident, and police found child pornography on his devices.

title: What’s next for Patriots’ offense after releasing Stefon Diggs?
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The New England Patriots are releasing Stefon Diggs, freeing up nearly $17 million in cap space.
Patriots are considering free agents Alec Pierce and A.J. Brown to fill Diggs' position.
New England may also explore the draft or trades for a new wide receiver in 2026.

title: Each mile is for her miracle: This Granby mom is running the Boston Marathon with her daughter in mind
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Brianna Poehler from Granby, Mass., is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for Boston Children's Hospital's Miles for Miracles.
Her daughter, a liver transplant survivor, received life-saving care at Boston Children's Hospital at 11 months old.
Poehler aims to raise funds for research and treatments supporting children through her marathon effort.
